message:
test: normalize floating-point tolerances to 1e-12 across ft-api and ft-autograd tests

The test suites used an inconsistent mix of epsilon thresholds (1e-4,
1e-6, 1e-10, 1e-15) for assertions that all compare against exact
mathematical identities (sin(0)=0, log1p(0)=0, expm1(0)=0, sigmoid,
tanh, BCE loss, smooth-L1 gradients, etc.).

Standardize on 1e-12 everywhere:
- Tighten the overly loose bounds (1e-4, 1e-6) in sigmoid, tanh, BCE
  loss, and smooth-L1 gradient assertions in ft-api.
- Relax the overly strict 1e-15 bounds (which can fail due to normal
  f64 rounding) to 1e-12 in log1p, expm1, and sin zero-point tests
  across both ft-api and ft-autograd.

No functional or production code changes — test-only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
